# 3D3D
> Offshore fabrication, marine hardware replacement, and discontinued part recovery. Founder-operated by Ken. Worldwide on-site deployment and remote engineering.

## Identity
3D3D is a specialty fabrication service built on twenty years of mechanical experience and offshore sailing. Ken is an offshore specialist with 20,000+ nautical miles, 10 years as a professional mechanic, and advanced 3D printing capability using engineering-grade compounds. The printer is the newest tool in a kit built to fix things that cannot fail at sea. Not a print farm. Not a hobby shop. A one-man fabrication operation that flies to the problem.

## Services
- [Marine Hardware](https://3d3d.ca/#what-i-do): Reverse-engineered deck fittings, fairleads, winch components, custom brackets for offshore dynamic loads
- [Automotive Restoration](https://3d3d.ca/#what-i-do): Discontinued OEM parts, interior trim, dash overlays, retaining clips for classic vehicles (AMC AMX, Dodge Polara, GTX, Roadrunner)
- [On-Site Deployment](https://3d3d.ca/#what-i-do): Worldwide travel with full equipment to marinas, boatyards, race support. The vessel cannot move, Ken can.
- [Remote Fabrication](https://3d3d.ca/#what-i-do): Send photos, measurements, and load requirements. Part designed and shipped to your coordinates.
- [Custom Quote](https://3d3d.ca/quote): Upload STL, 3MF, STEP, OBJ, PDF, or describe your idea
- [Print Shop](https://3d3d.ca/shop): Ready-made products for home, tools, automotive, marine, gifts
- [Book a Call](https://3d3d.ca/book): Schedule a consultation via Cal.com

## Materials (17 compounds)
### Engineering Tier
- ASA: UV-stable, 93°C HDT, above-deck marine hardware, outdoor. Acetone-smoothable.
- PETG-CF: Carbon fiber reinforced, structural brackets, low-creep, matte black.
- PC Blend: 113°C HDT, impact-resistant, tough at low temperatures.
- PC Blend CF: 114°C / 130°C annealed, stiff structural parts with heat performance.
- PP Glass Fiber: 138°C HDT, 0.11% moisture absorption, through-hull fittings, chemical-resistant.
- PP Carbon Fiber: 120°C HDT, near-zero moisture, lightweight structural.
- PA11 CF: 192°C HDT, engine bay compound, resists motor oils, fuels, benzene, ethanol.
- PC Space Grade: 137°C HDT, ESD-safe (6.0x10^7 ohm/sq), low-outgassing for space/vacuum.
- PETG V0: UL94 V0 flame retardant, self-extinguishing, electronics enclosures.

### Elite Tier
- PEI 1010 (Ultem-class): 207°C HDT, inherent VTM-0 flame retardancy, autoclave-sterilisable, metal-alloy replacement. Highest performance FDM compound.

### Flexible
- TPU 95A: Ether-type, hydrolysis-resistant, Shore 95A, impact resistant to -50°C. Seals, gaskets, vibration mounts.

### Specialty
- PETG Magnetite: 40% magnetite, paramagnetic, high-density.
- PETG Tungsten: 75% tungsten, ~19 g/cm3, radiation shielding, lead-free.

### Standard
- PETG: General-purpose functional, 68°C HDT.
- PLA: Prototyping only, 55°C HDT, not for end-use.
- PVB: IPA-smoothable to glass-like finish, transparent enclosures.

## Material Selection Guide
- Above deck, full sun: ASA
- Engine bay or exhaust proximity: PA11 CF
- Constant water contact / through-hull: PP Glass Fiber
- Structural bracket or mount: PETG-CF or PC Blend
- Seals, gaskets, vibration mounts: TPU 95A
- ESD-sensitive electronics: PC Space Grade
- Fire compliance required: PETG V0 or PEI 1010
- Prototype or fit check only: PLA
